entries.title: John 1 · The Eternal Word
paragraphs: The design of the evangelist is to show that in Christ was displayed the fullness of all blessings — that we may seek nothing elsewhere. This is why he begins not with the genealogy or the birth, but with the eternal generation of the Son: so that we may know that all things which relate to our salvation were present in him before the foundation of the world.
He calls him the Word because he is the express image of the invisible Father, the eternal Wisdom of God, and because through him God's will and counsel have been declared and executed from eternity.

entries.title: John 4 · The Samaritan Woman
paragraphs: Christ's departure from Judea is not flight but strategy. He who could have remained safely under the protection of the Father chooses the path of humility, withdrawing from the growing hostility of the Pharisees until the appointed hour.

The encounter at Jacob's Well illustrates the universal scope of Christ's mission. He who came to the lost sheep of the house of Israel does not pass by the Samaritans. The commission to preach the gospel to every creature was not first given after the resurrection; it was demonstrated in the ministry itself.

entries.title: Verse 10 — Living Water
paragraphs: Christ here accommodates his teaching to the capacity of his hearer. Beginning with water — that which she has come to draw — he leads her from the visible to the invisible, from the earthly to the heavenly. This is the true method of teaching: to begin where the learner is, and from thence to lift the mind upward.

The gift of God — by this expression Christ points to himself. He is God's gift to the world, the fountain of all spiritual blessings. To know this gift is the beginning of all true religion; ignorance of it is the source of all idolatry and self-righteousness.
